code:
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """Convert a list of airport‑related triples into a readable sentence."""
    if not triples:
        return ""
    # Helper to strip surrounding quotes from objects
    def _clean(obj: str) -> str:
        return obj.strip('\"')

    # Expanded fragment templates for many known predicates
    frag_templates = {
        "cityServed": "serves {object}",
        "location": "is located in {object}",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevel": "lies {object} metres above sea level",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInFeet": "lies {object} feet above sea level",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInMetres": "lies {object} metres above sea level",
        "operatingOrganisation": "is operated by {object}",
        "runwayLength": "has a runway length of {object} metres",
        "runwayName": "has runway {object}",
        "country": "is in {object}",
        "isPartOf": "is part of {object}",
        "1stRunwayLengthFeet": "has a first runway {object} feet long",
        "1stRunwayLengthMetre": "has a first runway {object} metres long",
        "1stRunwayNumber": "has runway number {object}",
        "runwaySurfaceType": "runway surface is {object}",
        "headquarter": "has its headquarter at {object}",
        "owner": "is owned by {object}",
        "nativeName": "is also known as {object}",
        "leaderParty": "is led by the {object} party",
        "capital": "has capital {object}",
        "language": "speaks {object}",
        "leader": "is led by {object}",
        "officialLanguage": "has official language {object}",
        "iataLocationIdentifier": "has IATA code {object}",
        "icaoLocationIdentifier": "has ICAO code {object}",
        "locationIdentifier": "has location identifier {object}",
        "postalCode": "has postal code {object}",
        "areaCode": "has area code {object}",
        "foundingYear": "was founded in {object}",
        "foundedBy": "was founded by {object}",
        "largestCity": "has largest city {object}",
        "countySeat": "has county seat {object}",
        "chief": "is headed by {object}",
        "jurisdiction": "lies within {object}",
        "demonym": "its residents are called {object}",
        "currency": "uses the {object}",
        # fallback for any other predicate
    }

    # Group fragments by subject
    subj_to_frags: dict[str, list[str]] = {}
    for t in triples:
        tmpl = frag_templates.get(t.predicate)
        if tmpl:
            frag = tmpl.format(object=_clean(t.object))
        else:
            # make predicate more readable
            pred_readable = t.predicate.replace("_", " ")
            frag = f"{pred_readable} {_clean(t.object)}"
        subj_to_frags.setdefault(t.subject, []).append(frag)

    # Build a clause for each subject
    clauses: list[str] = []
    for subj, frags in subj_to_frags.items():
        if not frags:
            continue
        if len(frags) == 1:
            clause = f"{subj} {frags[0]}"
        else:
            clause = f"{subj} " + ", ".join(frags[:-1]) + ", and " + frags[-1]
        clauses.append(clause)

    # Combine all subject clauses into one sentence
    if len(clauses) == 1:
        sentence = clauses[0] + "."
    else:
        sentence = ", ".join(clauses[:-1]) + ", and " + clauses[-1] + "."
    return sentence

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
